title: Rich Dense Chinese Pork Bao
description: A traditional Cantonese dish featuring rich pork filling and fresh mint, simmered to perfection in a savory broth, this recipe is a nostalgic treat for those who enjoy dense and umami flavors.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine chopped pork shoulder, mint leaves, scallions, and grated ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil, then pour the mixture over the pork mixture and mix well.
Knead the bao dough according to the package instructions or recipe, then divide it into 8-10 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle, then place a tablespoon of the pork filling in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, then press the edges together to seal the bao.
Place the bao in a steamer lined with parchment paper, leaving some space between each bao to allow for expansion.
Steam the bao over boiling water for 15-20 minutes, or until they are cooked through and fluffy.
Serve the bao hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
